summary:
During an initial encounter at what seems likely to be their first dental check-up, the boy displays symptoms indicative primarily of situational anxiety—such as avoidance behavior through repetitive motions and sudden outbursts—which do not point toward more severe conditions but instead reflect normal developmental stressors encountered by some preschoolers when exposed to new environments or procedures. \boxed{A} This summary captures both critical aspects—the specific nature of potential triggers based on location—and focuses directly on ruling out other less probable causes while highlighting that standard childhood anxieties appear highly consistent with observed behaviors during this particular event. 

The final answer is therefore $\boxed{\text{A}}$.
